I think stories like this are really good in vr, you can really get a feeling of what a place is like!
The experience was amazing and am lucky I could speak Arabic and so I understood every thing and the rocket scared the crap out of me
Shows an inside personal view of the hardship and tragedy.. Very eye opening. Just wish it was longer film.
More real than expected. An excellent opportunity to see and understand the lives of our oppressed brothers on the other end of Earth.

I have the same type of friends growing up the atmosphere of their house was just like so many families I visited,  I'm sad for what happened to them, I hope they recover from this tragic loss they faced.
And they get a better life to live in.
WAR is never a good thing to face including family disputes no matter what the outcome was.

reviews.review_title: To those who 'thought it was a game',
reviews.review_description:
Don't even bother to review.  It clearly says in the description 'not a game', so don't throw it under the bus because you were too lazy to read.  Even those who gave it a high rating call it a game,   No..it's not a game.  It's a documentary.  If you were bummed about not being able to shoot baddies in Fallujah or throw grenades at Isis and had to hear the 'boring' man's story about how he lost 2 members of his family to terrorism, then you sir,m'aam clearly have the attention span of a gnat and should really stick to playing the old xbox until your brains turn into tapioca.  It's even free.  Don't accept free butterscotch candy from someone and then piss and moan how you don't like butterscotch.  

Now that's out of the way, I enjoyed the DOCUMENTARY a lot.  It seemed a little unfinished in parts where the dad was in mid sentence and cut out or you couldnt go upstairs.  I hope more content like this gets developed for VR and the stupid 1 star reviews don't prevent that from happening.   We have enough mindless shoot'em up games, thanks.
It says not a game don’t lazy just read it. It is sad I cried I liked it.

Please change the way we interact with the tour guide. I jumped every time he appeared. You're in silent environment where nothing's happening and then he jumps out of nowhere. Additionally you have doors that suddenly fly open. It really kept me from taking in the story. I would recommend having a floating icon in the room that The player can push to activate the narrator.

As witnessed all too briefly at the end of "The Key", photogrammetry is a powerful tool for photorealistic results on the Quest. For experiences like this, realistic graphics are more than about impressing the viewer - they're about actually feeling like you've stepped into a new environment, hammering home the message that what you're seeing happened to real people. Very well done indeed. The only thing I felt was lacking was an ability to go back -  sometimes I wasn't sure if I'd missed something.

In response to Gamer 217, believe it or not, we don’t all live in the United States, a fact that a lot of people are extremely grateful about. Back to this VR experience. Very well put together and a must download for all quest users. Whatever country you’re from.

I really enjoyed the experience.
A little bit clunky here and there but that almost adds to the character.

Without spoiling anything I agree with the others... You would have to have a heart of stone not to shed a tear.

Definitely worth a download.
Honestly this is pretty insane it’s a wholesome monk pretty sad to, This was also the type of game that I want to see
It’s a touching story and these other reviews are so bad but Rip to his sons

“Nothing is enjoyable to me anymore; nothing tastes sweet anymore.” 
This was my introduction to this powerful, tragic story, representative of the experiences of too many innocent people in areas of war.
Thank you to those who created this experience, especially to Mr. Ahmaied Khalaf who shared his family’s personal trauma in the interest of making the world a better place. 
It’s not easy to watch, nor should it be. Anyone with a roof over their head and stable circumstances has an obligation to watch this unique VR experience.
I cried. It’s very moving. Download this now and wait for a moment to watch it when you are ready for something very heavy. 
This is happening in the world. We need to work toward peace globally. Those of us living in peaceful conditions cannot stick our heads in the sand. 
My heart goes out to this family.

Very immersive and impactful experience

If I could make a request to the developers, could you boost the audio please? At maximum volume on the Quest 2, the narration in the house was barely audible to me, and the narration during the video segments were entirely inaudible altogether.
I did not expect this to be so moving and intense.
This game,is so sad because his son died and It had me crying after it’s so sad.
It is a series of 360 video. I thought the message was not as well done as "the key" and I was quickly bored

The story in really touching and I fill really sad for what happened to that family.
This is an interactive VR movie experience where you can walk in the scene.
I'm looking for more experience like that and trying to develop mine!

reviews.review_title: Didn’t expect it to make me cry.
reviews.review_description: This is my first review and rightfully so. This is a beautifully told story that is very engaging and helps you understand what is really going on in the Middle East and how the families are affected by it. I was overcome by emotions and couldn’t stop myself from crying. I would absolutely recommend this experience!
